description: Fantasy: Stories set in imaginary worlds with magical elements.
Science Fiction: Stories exploring futuristic concepts, advanced technology, or space travel.
Romance: Stories focused on love and emotional relationships.
Horror: Stories intended to frighten or disturb the audience.
Mystery: Stories centered on solving a crime or uncovering secrets.
Thriller: Stories designed to create suspense and excitement.
Historical Fiction: Stories set in a past time period with accurate historical details.
Biography: Non-fiction works telling the life story of a person.
Action: Stories or games emphasizing physical activity, fights, and stunts.
Comedy: Stories or films intended to entertain and provoke laughter.
Drama: Stories focusing on emotional and relational development of characters.
Romantic Comedy: Light-hearted stories combining romance and humor.
Documentary: Works documenting real events, people, or phenomena.
Role-Playing Game: Games where players control characters in a fictional world, often with story progression.
First-Person Shooter: Games focused on shooting from the protagonist's perspective.
Third-Person Shooter: Shooting games viewed from a camera behind the character.
Strategy: Games emphasizing planning, tactics, and resource management.
Simulation: Games that replicate real-world activities or scenarios.
Adventure: Games or stories focusing on exploration and puzzle-solving.
Sports: Games simulating real-world sports competitions.
Racing: Games centered on competitive racing of vehicles or characters.
Fighting: Games focused on combat between characters in arenas or stages.
Musical: Works where songs and music play a central role in storytelling.
Crime: Stories revolving around criminal activities and investigations.
Detective: Stories focusing on a detective solving cases and mysteries.
War: Stories or films portraying conflicts, battles, and the human impact of war.
Animation: Works created using animated visuals, often aimed at family audiences.
Sandbox: Games giving players freedom to explore and create in an open world.
Horror Game: Games designed to frighten players through suspense and disturbing content.
Psychological Thriller: Works focused on suspense, mind games, and mental states.
Cyberpunk: Stories or games set in dystopian futures with advanced technology and social decay.
Steampunk: Works blending historical settings with steam-powered technology and imaginative inventions.
Slice of Life: Stories depicting everyday experiences and personal growth.
Metroidvania: Games emphasizing exploration and unlocking new areas as abilities grow.
Survival: Games or stories focusing on staying alive under harsh conditions.
Platformer: Games where players navigate levels by jumping, climbing, or running.
Open World: Games allowing players to freely explore large virtual worlds.
Western: Stories set in the American Old West, often featuring cowboys, outlaws, and frontier life.
Noir: Dark, cynical stories with morally ambiguous characters, often in crime settings.
Political Thriller: Stories involving political conspiracies, espionage, and intrigue.
Dark Fantasy: Fantasy stories with horror elements, grim themes, and moral ambiguity.
Epic Fantasy: Fantasy stories with large-scale conflicts, complex worlds, and numerous characters.
Dystopian: Stories set in oppressive or futuristic societies, often critiquing social structures.
